---
description: "SQLAlchemy model conventions, PostgreSQL schema rules, and Alembic migration standards. Apply when adding or modifying models, migrations, or SQL."
name: "Data Model and Schema"
applyTo:
  - "app/models/**/*.py"
  - "migrations/**/*.py"
  - "**/*.sql"
---
# Data Model and Schema Rules

## Primary Keys

- All tables use UUID primary keys generated by `gen_random_uuid()` in PostgreSQL.
- Never use auto-increment integer PKs for domain entities.

## Timestamps

- Every table includes `created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()` and `updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()`.
- Always use `TIMESTAMPTZ` (timezone-aware). Never use `TIMESTAMP WITHOUT TIME ZONE`.

## Monetary Values

- Store all monetary amounts as `NUMERIC(18,2)`. Never use `FLOAT`, `REAL`, or `DOUBLE PRECISION` for money.
- Include a `currency_code CHAR(3)` column alongside any monetary amount column.

## JSONB Usage

- JSONB is permitted only for sparse metadata, provider-specific config, raw lineage snapshots, and mapping rule storage.
- Never use JSONB for core reporting metrics, KPIs, or any field that will be filtered, aggregated, or sorted in reports.
- Reporting fact tables must use strongly typed columns (`NUMERIC`, `BIGINT`, `DATE`, `TEXT`).

## Schema Changes

- All schema changes go through Alembic migrations. Never run `CREATE TABLE`, `ALTER TABLE`, or `DROP TABLE` manually against any environment.
- Migration scripts must be reversible (`upgrade` and `downgrade` both implemented).
- Migration file names must be descriptive: `<revision>_<short_description>.py`.

## Required Indexes

- Add an index on every foreign key column.
- Add a composite index on `(organization_id, metric_date)` for all fact and time-series tables.
- Add a business-key dedupe index `(organization_id, source_system, external_campaign_id, metric_date)` on `fact_marketing_metric_daily`.
- Add GIN indexes on JSONB columns that will be searched with `@>` or `?` operators.

## Canonical Fact Table Conventions

- `fact_marketing_metric_daily` rows must include `source_import_job_id` FK for full lineage traceability.
- `organization_id` must appear on every tenant-scoped table for workspace isolation.
- Metrics columns (`spend`, `impressions`, `clicks`, etc.) are nullable where a given source may not provide the value.

## Definition Of Done

- Every new table has UUID PK, `created_at`, `updated_at`, and an Alembic migration.
- No money stored in float types.
- No JSONB used for filterable or aggregatable reporting fields.
- Every FK column has an index.
